comment: Sajtbyggaren OpenClaw action registry. OpenClaw is a conductor on the controlled engine, not a free agent. status: supported | partial | planned.

actions.note: Colour/font/theme -> brand/tone via theme_directives; compound colours via the stylist role + color_lexicon.
name/tagline/about/services via copyDirective; LLM-understood (copyDirectiveModel) with deterministic guards.

Mounts a sanctioned section's capability + dossier via the apply chain (router section_add -> resolve type to capability with an implementing dossier -> apply requestedCapabilities + selectedDossiers.required -> targeted render -> new immutable version). Nine sanctioned types as of 2026-06-08 (commit 4c6ba67): team, faq, trust/guarantees, reviews, gallery, pricing, hours, location (map), contact-form. VISIBLE TYPES (flipped 2026-06-09): faq and team now render VISIBLY on the local-service-business scaffold by surfacing a grounded dedicated route (/faq via render_faq, /team via render_team) through the existing wizard extra-route plumbing (apply records the wizard mustHave label on the next version's meta sidecar). appliedVisibleEffect=true ONLY on a real render: faq is grounded by construction (areas/opening-hours answers), team requires grounded company.team (an empty team stays honest mounted-but-no-content). Non-LSB scaffolds keep faq/team mount-only until their renderer set opts into wizard routes. CONTACT-FORM (B198 del b, 2026-06-12): renders VISIBLY on the ecommerce-lite scaffold by surfacing the EXISTING /kontakt route (no new page) when the resend-contact-form hard dossier is mounted (render_contact injects <ResendContactForm>), so affectedRoutes=[contact] + appliedVisibleEffect=true; gated narrowly on CONTACT_FORM_VISIBLE_SCAFFOLDS={ecommerce-lite} AND the mounted resend dossier. The mailto default has no visible component and stays mount-only, as does contact-form on any other scaffold. INLINE TYPES: hours (ADR 0038) and gallery (ADR 0042, 2026-06-10) render INLINE on home via directives.mountedSections on local-service-business + ecommerce-lite, honouring the router position (top/bottom); gallery, which is already part of the default home order when gallery images exist, is MOVED to the explicit position instead of duplicated. Both are gated on grounded content (contact.openingHours resp. uploaded gallery images) — otherwise honestly mount-only. MOUNT-ONLY (still): trust/guarantees, reviews, pricing, location mount the dossier but render no new section yet (applied=true, appliedVisibleEffect=false) — pricing/location can follow the same inline pattern next. Unknown/unsupported type (incl. hero/services/cta-banner) = honest no-op.

Owned by the component_builder role (ADR 0057), grounded in the Component Catalog (ADR 0040: capability-map components + per-Starter component-manifest). SUPPORTED with a mount-only default and TWO visible exceptions (Generative Component V1, ADR 0061/0064): the image-placeholder-grid recipe ('lägg till 6 bildplatshållare', 'lägg till en bildgrid') and cta-contact-block recipe ('lägg till en kontaktknapp', 'lägg till en kontaktruta', 'boka', 'offert', 'cta') MATERIALISE VISIBLY by writing a new components/generated/<id>.tsx Server Component (deterministic template, NO LLM-generated free code, NO new npm deps) spliced into the target route's page.tsx before the closing </main>, through the SAME build + Quality Gate + immutable-versioning pipeline (a QG/build failure never swaps current.json). The CTA recipe renders a generic contact/booking CTA without inventing phone, email or route facts. Flow: router classifies the recipe noun as component_add (componentIntent=image_placeholder_grid or contact_button) -> generative_component_directives.resolve_generative_component parses the count when relevant (image grid clamps 1..12/default 6; CTA is fixed to 1) and resolves routeId (default home) or REFUSES a non-whitelisted generative family -> apply records directives.generativeComponents (STICKY: union by id) -> build_site materialises after write_pages via packages.generation.codegen.followup_emit (fail-closed write-path guards: never outside the build dir, never package.json/.env/node_modules; idempotent). MOUNT-ONLY (still): every non-recipe component_add gets a catalog-grounded answer or an HONEST no-op (stage generative_unsupported for a recognised-but-unsupported generative family like a carousel) - it mounts nothing and writes no files, and the chain reports the no-op via unappliedFollowupIntents, never an invented component. Free LLM codegen for arbitrary components is a later slice; vendoring a wholly new component type stays an operator PR (intake -> review -> Starter).
Reorder/move sections; needs an apply capability, not free CSS.

Route/Nav Mutation V1 (ADR 0060), route_editor role - the NON-DESTRUCTIVE sibling of route_remove. Hides a page's header/footer nav LINK while KEEPING the page, via a structured directive (directives.hiddenNavRoutes), NOT a dossier and NOT a free file patch. Flow: router classifies 'dölj Om oss i menyn / ta bort Om oss ur menyn / ta bort länken till Kontakt' as nav_hide and resolves a best-effort routeId from the page label -> route_directives.resolve_hidden_nav_routes validates the id against THIS site's scaffold defaultRoutes (home is always kept in nav; unknown/already-hidden are refused) -> apply records directives.hiddenNavRoutes (STICKY: union with the prior version) -> build_site threads hidden ids to write_pages -> render_layout -> _nav_items_from_scaffold, which drops ONLY the (href,label) nav item in one seam. Unlike route_remove the page.tsx is still written, route-guards/Quality Gate still count the page and _pick_contact_route still finds the contact route. A nav-only request that names no resolvable page ('ta bort länken') stays an honest component_remove no-op, never nav_hide. Skill shared with route_remove (route_editor role owns both).

Route/Nav Mutation V1 (ADR 0060). Removes a whole non-required page + its header/footer nav link via a structured directive (directives.disabledRoutes), NOT a dossier and NOT a free file patch. Flow: router classifies 'ta bort sidan X / radera X-sidan / ta bort Kontakt' as route_remove and resolves a best-effort routeId from the page label -> route_directives.resolve_disabled_routes validates the id against THIS site's scaffold defaultRoutes + the required-page guard (returns refused reasons for an unknown or required page) -> apply records directives.disabledRoutes (STICKY: union with the prior version so a removed page never reappears) -> build_site.py computes activeRoutes = scaffold defaultRoutes minus disabledRoutes in one seam, so the page.tsx is never written and the nav drops the link -> new immutable version + targeted render. Scaffold-agnostic (works on every starter that has a non-required route). Slice A keeps REQUIRED pages (home/services/contact) - removing contact + retargeting its CTAs to mailto/tel/none, plus a Quality Gate dangling-link scan, is Slice B. An unknown/required page is an HONEST no-op (stage route_remove_unsupported), never a faked removal. Wizard-extra routes (faq/team/pricing surfaced via wizardMustHave) are not scaffold defaultRoutes, so removing them is a later slice.
Read-only answer/critique from artifacts; never builds.
